message:
perf(sse): track scanned_len to skip re-scanning already-processed buffer bytes

Add a scanned_len field to SseParser that tracks how many bytes of the
internal buffer have already been searched for newline characters. This
prevents redundant memchr2 scans over previously-processed data when
new chunks are appended to an incomplete line in the buffer.

The optimization matters for streaming scenarios where small chunks
arrive frequently and the buffer accumulates partial lines:

- Fast path (empty buffer): scanned_len is reset to 0 or set to the
  length of any remaining tail pushed to the buffer
- Slow path (buffer has data): new data is appended, then scanning
  starts from scanned_len minus 1 (the minus-1 handles the edge case
  where a CRLF pair was split across the old buffer tail and new data)
- After draining consumed bytes, scanned_len is set to the remaining
  buffer length since that tail was just scanned

Also passes scan_start into process_source() so the memchr2 search
begins at the correct offset rather than re-scanning from byte 0,
and adjusts BOM handling to account for the search_pos parameter.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
